# Qwidgets ‚Üí LightNap Upstream Diff Report

**Generated:** 2026-05-22
**Generated by:** Claude Code (analysis-only run; no source files modified)

> **2026-05-23 update ‚Äî project-layout refactor landed.** This commit adopts the Qwidgets `src/`
> folder grouping and introduces per-vendor OAuth integration libraries hub'd through
> `LightNap.Configuration`. See the correspondence map in ¬ß1 ‚Äî LightNap now mirrors that layout:
> `src/DataProviders/`, `src/WebJobs/LightNap.MaintenanceService/`, and
> `src/Integrations/{GitHub,Google,Microsoft}/`. The Web API no longer references vendor OAuth
> NuGets directly (`AspNet.Security.OAuth.GitHub`, `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authentication.Google`,
> `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authentication.MicrosoftAccount` are gone from `LightNap.WebApi.csproj`);
> the Configuration project references each `LightNap.Integrations.*` project and
> `AddLightNapOAuthProviders(...)` dispatches to each vendor's `Add{Vendor}Login` extension.
> Azure plumbing (DataProtection, Storage, Redis) stayed coupled into the core projects per the
> "local dev + Azure production" assumption. Marks BE-01 effectively complete on the LightNap side.

### Baseline-pinning evidence

- The two repos share no git history (Qwidgets's first commit is `fa337e6`, *Initial commit*, with 1023 files and 94 910 insertions ‚Äî all of LightNap's tree dropped into a fresh repo).
- LightNap@HEAD is dated **2025-12-15**; Qwidgets's initial commit is **2026-01-08** ‚Äî *Qwidgets was forked ~3.5 weeks after LightNap@HEAD*.
- Triangulating dependencies confirms this: Qwidgets's initial commit already contains `src/qwidgets-ng/orval.config.js`, which was added to LightNap in commit `4d348c3` (#62, "Use orval to sync frontend code with backend APIs", 2025-12-15). It also contains the rate-limiting subsystem (#56), OAuth (#59), and `v21 with NuGet, Angular, and PrimeNG updates` (#61) ‚Äî every LightNap PR up to and including HEAD.
- LightNap has **not** moved forward since the fork (`git log --before="2026-01-09"` returns exactly the same range as `git log` against HEAD).
- Consequence: `diff(LightNap@baseline ‚Üí LightNap@HEAD)` is **empty**. Every difference between `LightNap@HEAD` and `Qwidgets@HEAD` is a Qwidgets-authored change; nothing in this report risks "already upstream" or "conflicts with LightNap's later evolution." Findings are **High confidence** on direction unless otherwise noted.
- **Temporary remote.** `qwidgets-tmp` (pointing at the local Qwidgets repo) was added to the LightNap repo during analysis so trees were reachable from one place. **It is removed at the end of this run** (see closing note). No checkouts were performed.

## 1. Project / folder correspondence map

LightNap's `src/` layout vs. Qwidgets's `src/` layout. Every later finding references paths from this map.

| LightNap (baseline) | Qwidgets (HEAD) | Status / Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `src/LightNap.Core/` | `src/Qwidgets.Core/` | Renamed; many sub-areas same, several Qwidgets-only subfolders (see below). |
| `src/LightNap.WebApi/` | `src/Qwidgets.WebApi/` | Renamed; restructured (most DI logic moved out to `Qwidgets.Configuration` and `Extensions/IServiceCollectionExtensions.cs`). |
| `src/LightNap.Core.Tests/` | `src/Qwidgets.Core.Tests/` | Renamed; now also references `Qwidgets.WebApi` so middleware/etc. can be tested. |
| `src/LightNap.DataProviders.SqlServer/` | `src/DataProviders/Qwidgets.DataProviders.SqlServer/` | Renamed **and relocated** under a new `DataProviders/` parent. |
| `src/LightNap.DataProviders.Sqlite/` | `src/DataProviders/Qwidgets.DataProviders.Sqlite/` | Renamed and relocated. |
| `src/LightNap.MaintenanceService/` | `src/WebJobs/Qwidgets.MaintenanceService/` | Renamed **and relocated** under a new `WebJobs/` parent (alongside per-integration WebJobs). |
| `src/Scaffolding/` | *(removed)* | Qwidgets deleted the scaffolding tool & templates entirely. Not an upstream candidate (removal of LightNap's own subsystem, not an improvement). |
| `src/lightnap-ng/` | `src/qwidgets-ng/` | Renamed Angular project. |
| *(does not exist)* | **`src/Qwidgets.Configuration/`** | **New project** ‚Äî extraction of shared config models + DI extensions. Several pieces are generic; see BE-01. |
| `src/Integrations/{Anthropic,Github,Google,Microsoft}/` | `src/Integrations/{Anthropic,Github,Google,Microsoft}/` | Renamed-only (same generic OAuth/LLM-integration libraries; structure preserved). |
| *(does not exist)* | `src/Integrations/{Kalshi,Polymarket,PredictIt,Manifold}/` + their `*.{Provider}WebJob/` siblings | **Domain-specific** (prediction-market exchanges + per-market WebJobs). Excluded from candidates. |
| *(does not exist)* | `src/Qwidgets.Core/Ai/`, `Predictions/`, `Workspaces/`, `SharedWorkspaces/`, `Integrations/`, `Telemetry/`, `OpenAPIs/` | **Domain-specific** subfolders inside Core. Excluded. |

Folders **only in LightNap** that have no Qwidgets counterpart (LightNap-side additions that Qwidgets did not adopt):

- `src/LightNap.Core/Public/` (empty placeholder `IPublicService`/`PublicService` stubs ‚Äî Qwidgets removed them as unused).
- `src/LightNap.Core/Demo/` (mentioned in folder listing; no diff data ‚Äî likely empty/demo placeholder).
- `src/LightNap.Core/TestEntities/` and `Products/` exist in both repos.
- `src/LightNap.WebApi/Filters/IncludeBaseClassSchemasFilter.cs` (Swashbuckle helper; Qwidgets removed it).

These are LightNap-only and therefore **not candidates for upstreaming** (you can't upstream a removal *to yourself*). They are noted only for completeness.

Open question for the maintainer:

> **OQ-1.** Did Qwidgets's wholesale removal of the `Scaffolding/` subsystem reflect a deprecation decision that should also be made in LightNap, or was scaffolding simply unused in Qwidgets's workflow? If the latter, LightNap should keep `Scaffolding/`. No upstream action either way.

## 3. Backend

#### BE-01 ‚Äî Extract `Qwidgets.Configuration` as a reusable config library

- **Area:** Cross-cutting (Core, WebApi, MaintenanceService).
- **What changed:** Qwidgets created a new `Qwidgets.Configuration` project that hosts strongly-typed settings (`DatabaseSettings`, `DatabaseProvider`, `CacheSettings`, `DataProtectionSettings`, `SupportedOAuthProviderSettings`) and the `IServiceCollectionExtensions` / `IConfigurationExtensions` that bind / validate them. The WebApi and MaintenanceService both depend on it. The same idea (one library, multiple hosts) lets LightNap stop duplicating bootstrap code between WebApi and MaintenanceService.
- **Why generic:** Pure refactor ‚Äî same shape that LightNap has today, just in one place. Not domain.
- **Files:** New project [src/Qwidgets.Configuration/](src/Qwidgets.Configuration/) (~20 files). Compare against the LightNap originals: [src/LightNap.Core/Extensions/ApplicationServiceExtensions.cs](src/LightNap.Core/Extensions/ApplicationServiceExtensions.cs), [src/LightNap.Core/Extensions/IConfigurationExtensions.cs](src/LightNap.Core/Extensions/IConfigurationExtensions.cs), [src/LightNap.WebApi/Extensions/ApplicationServiceExtensions.cs](src/LightNap.WebApi/Extensions/ApplicationServiceExtensions.cs), [src/LightNap.WebApi/Configuration/CacheSettings.cs](src/LightNap.WebApi/Configuration/CacheSettings.cs), [src/LightNap.Core/Configuration/Database/DatabaseSettings.cs](src/LightNap.Core/Configuration/Database/DatabaseSettings.cs).
- **Classification:** Gray-area (it's a big refactor that touches the project layout; the *concept* is generic but the actual code in `Qwidgets.Configuration` is entangled with Qwidgets-specific bits ‚Äî `PredictionSearchSnapshotStorageSettings`, `PredictionSearchRefreshSettings`, and the `BlobPredictionSearchSnapshot*` services. The maintainer would have to split the generic core (database / data-protection / OAuth provider / cache settings) from the domain ones).
- **Recommendation:** Needs maintainer judgment. If you like the idea, the clean form for upstream is `LightNap.Configuration` containing only: `CacheSettings`, `DatabaseSettings`, `DatabaseProvider`, `DataProtectionSettings`/sub-types, `SupportedOAuthProviderSettings`, `IConfigurationExtensions`, and the generic-only DI methods.
- **Confidence:** High that the refactor is conceptually generic; Medium that the maintainer wants this much project churn.
- **Risk & effort:** Medium-high; touches sln + project refs in WebApi, MaintenanceService, Tests.
- **Depends on:** BE-02, BE-03, BE-07, BE-08, BE-10, BE-19, DEP-08.

**DECISION:** `[x] upstream`

#### BE-02 ‚Äî Builder-style `AddXxxServices(...)` with optional `ILogger` for startup diagnostics

- **Area:** DI/bootstrap.
- **What changed:** Qwidgets's `IServiceCollectionExtensions` accepts an `ILogger? logger = null` parameter on each `AddQwidgetsDatabaseServices` / `AddQwidgetsEmailServices` / `AddQwidgetsIdentityServices` / `AddQwidgetsLogToConsoleEmailSender` / `AddQwidgetsSmtpEmailSender` / etc., and logs what it's wiring up (provider name, masked connection string).
- **Why generic:** LightNap today silently wires services in `AddDatabaseServices(...)` / `AddEmailServices(...)`. Logging *what was wired* during startup is invaluable when debugging misconfigured envs.
- **Files:** New file [src/Qwidgets.Configuration/Extensions/IServiceCollectionExtensions.cs](src/Qwidgets.Configuration/Extensions/IServiceCollectionExtensions.cs) (compare to LightNap [src/LightNap.Core/Extensions/ApplicationServiceExtensions.cs](src/LightNap.Core/Extensions/ApplicationServiceExtensions.cs):1-46 and [src/LightNap.WebApi/Extensions/ApplicationServiceExtensions.cs](src/LightNap.WebApi/Extensions/ApplicationServiceExtensions.cs)). Bootstrap consumer pattern: [src/LightNap.WebApi/Program.cs](src/LightNap.WebApi/Program.cs).
- **Classification:** Generic.
- **Recommendation:** Upstream (without BE-01's project extraction if simpler ‚Äî add the `ILogger?` param + log statements in-place).
- **Confidence:** High.
- **Risk & effort:** Low-medium; non-breaking if `logger` is optional.
- **Depends on:** BE-16.

**DECISION:** `[x] upstream`

#### BE-03 ‚Äî Add extensible `DataProtection` services (Azure Key Vault / Local cert / Ephemeral)

- **Area:** ASP.NET Core data protection.
- **What changed:** New `DataProtectionSettings` (Provider enum + Azure / Local sub-records) and `AddQwidgetsDataProtectionServices(...)` switching between `PersistKeysToAzureBlobStorage + ProtectKeysWithAzureKeyVault`, `PersistKeysToFileSystem + ProtectKeysWithCertificate` (X.509 subject lookup in CurrentUser store), and `UseEphemeralDataProtectionProvider()`. Wired through `appsettings.json` with a `"DataProtection"` section. E2e tests use `Ephemeral`.
- **Why generic:** LightNap today relies on the default in-memory data-protection key ring. This is a real production gap ‚Äî distributed/Azure deployments need a persisted, protected key ring or auth cookies / antiforgery tokens stop working across instances. The settings shape is fully generic.
- **Files:** [src/Qwidgets.Configuration/Models/DataProtection/DataProtectionSettings.cs](src/Qwidgets.Configuration/Models/DataProtection/DataProtectionSettings.cs), [src/Qwidgets.Configuration/Models/DataProtection/AzureDataProtectionSettings.cs](src/Qwidgets.Configuration/Models/DataProtection/AzureDataProtectionSettings.cs), [src/Qwidgets.Configuration/Models/DataProtection/LocalDataProtectionSettings.cs](src/Qwidgets.Configuration/Models/DataProtection/LocalDataProtectionSettings.cs), [src/Qwidgets.Configuration/Models/DataProtection/DataProtectionProvider.cs](src/Qwidgets.Configuration/Models/DataProtection/DataProtectionProvider.cs), DI method in [src/Qwidgets.Configuration/Extensions/IServiceCollectionExtensions.cs](src/Qwidgets.Configuration/Extensions/IServiceCollectionExtensions.cs) (search `AddQwidgetsDataProtectionServices`). Consumer: [src/Qwidgets.WebApi/Program.cs](src/Qwidgets.WebApi/Program.cs). New `"DataProtection"` keys in [src/Qwidgets.WebApi/appsettings.json](src/Qwidgets.WebApi/appsettings.json), [appsettings.E2e.json](src/Qwidgets.WebApi/appsettings.E2e.json), [appsettings.Production.json](src/Qwidgets.WebApi/appsettings.Production.json), and the MaintenanceService [appsettings.json](src/WebJobs/Qwidgets.MaintenanceService/appsettings.json).
- **Classification:** Generic. **Flagged as security-relevant.**
- **Recommendation:** Upstream.
- **Confidence:** High.
- **Risk & effort:** Medium; new NuGets (`Azure.Identity`, `Azure.Storage.Blobs` ‚Äî see DEP-08), new appsettings section, optional cert/Vault per environment.
- **Depends on:** DEP-08, BE-02.

**DECISION:** `[x] upstream`

#### BE-04 ‚Äî Mask connection strings in startup logs

- **Area:** DataProviders (SqlServer + Sqlite).
- **What changed:** `MaskConnectionString` helpers in each provider's `IServiceCollectionExtensions.cs` use `SqlConnectionStringBuilder` / `SqliteConnectionStringBuilder` to log `Server=X;Database=Y;***` and `Data Source=X;***` rather than the raw string with credentials.
- **Why generic:** Trivially generic security improvement (don't put passwords in logs).
- **Files:** [src/DataProviders/Qwidgets.DataProviders.SqlServer/Extensions/IServiceCollectionExtensions.cs](src/DataProviders/Qwidgets.DataProviders.SqlServer/Extensions/IServiceCollectionExtensions.cs):20-30, [src/DataProviders/Qwidgets.DataProviders.Sqlite/Extensions/IServiceCollectionExtensions.cs](src/DataProviders/Qwidgets.DataProviders.Sqlite/Extensions/IServiceCollectionExtensions.cs):25-35. Compare to LightNap [src/LightNap.DataProviders.SqlServer/Extensions/ApplicationServiceExtensions.cs](src/LightNap.DataProviders.SqlServer/Extensions/ApplicationServiceExtensions.cs) and the Sqlite equivalent (~23 lines each, no masking).
- **Classification:** Generic. **Flagged as security-relevant.**
- **Recommendation:** Upstream.
- **Confidence:** High.
- **Risk & effort:** Very low.
- **Depends on:** BE-02 (paired with the `ILogger?` parameter).

**DECISION:** `[x] upstream`

#### BE-05 ‚Äî Switch `NotificationsHub` to a strongly-typed SignalR client (`RealTimeHub` / `IRealTimeClient`)

- **Area:** SignalR.
- **What changed:** Qwidgets removed `NotificationsHub` and added `IRealTimeClient` (interface defining `ReceiveNotificationAsync(...)`) and `RealTimeHub : Hub<IRealTimeClient>`. `NotificationService` now takes `IHubContext<RealTimeHub, IRealTimeClient>` and replaces the stringly-typed `Clients.Group(...).SendAsync("ReceiveNotification", dto)` with an extension method `SendNotificationToUserAsync(userId, dto)`.
- **Why generic:** Strongly-typed SignalR hub clients are the recommended best practice in ASP.NET Core ‚Äî typo-resistant, refactor-safe.
- **Files:** New [src/Qwidgets.Core/Hubs/RealTimeHub.cs](src/Qwidgets.Core/Hubs/RealTimeHub.cs), [src/Qwidgets.Core/Hubs/IRealTimeClient.cs](src/Qwidgets.Core/Hubs/IRealTimeClient.cs), [src/Qwidgets.Core/Extensions/SignalRClientExtensions.cs](src/Qwidgets.Core/Extensions/SignalRClientExtensions.cs); removed [src/LightNap.Core/Hubs/NotificationsHub.cs](src/LightNap.Core/Hubs/NotificationsHub.cs); updated [src/Qwidgets.Core/Notifications/Services/NotificationService.cs](src/Qwidgets.Core/Notifications/Services/NotificationService.cs):1-20 (param + `SendNotificationToUserAsync`); hub mount in [src/Qwidgets.WebApi/Program.cs](src/Qwidgets.WebApi/Program.cs) (`/api/hubs/realtime` instead of `/api/hubs/notifications`).
- **Classification:** Generic.
- **Recommendation:** Upstream. *Note: the hub URL changes (`/api/hubs/notifications` ‚Üí `/api/hubs/realtime`) ‚Äî the frontend hub client URL must change in lockstep.*
- **Confidence:** High.
- **Risk & effort:** Low-medium; coordinate with frontend `NotificationHubService`.
- **Depends on:** none (but frontend `NotificationHubService` URL is the paired change).

**DECISION:** `[x] upstream`

#### BE-06 ‚Äî Bug fix: always set `MailMessage.IsBodyHtml = true` in `DefaultEmailService`

- **Area:** Email.
- **What changed:** In LightNap [DefaultEmailService.SendMailAsync(MailMessage)](src/LightNap.Core/Email/Services/DefaultEmailService.cs):10-13, `IsBodyHtml` is **not** set ‚Äî so any caller that constructs the `MailMessage` themselves (the `SendChangeEmailAsync` path does this directly) bypasses HTML rendering and the email arrives as raw HTML in plain-text form. Qwidgets's [DefaultEmailService](src/Qwidgets.Core/Email/Services/DefaultEmailService.cs):10-18 fixes this by setting `message.IsBodyHtml = true` at the entry point and routing all sends through that single method (`SendChangeEmailAsync` now also flows through `this.SendMailAsync(...)` instead of calling `emailSender.SendMailAsync(...)` directly).
- **Why generic:** Real bug; affects every consumer of LightNap.
- **Files:** [src/LightNap.Core/Email/Services/DefaultEmailService.cs](src/LightNap.Core/Email/Services/DefaultEmailService.cs):10-13, 45-58.
- **Classification:** Generic. **Bug fix.**
- **Recommendation:** Upstream.
- **Confidence:** High.
- **Risk & effort:** Very low.
- **Depends on:** none.

**DECISION:** `[x] upstream`

#### BE-14 ‚Äî Static-file cache-control headers (no-cache for `index.html`/`ngsw*`, immutable for hashed assets, no-cache for StaticContent)

- **Area:** WebApi static file middleware.
- **What changed:** [src/Qwidgets.WebApi/Program.cs](src/Qwidgets.WebApi/Program.cs) (search `OnPrepareResponse`) attaches an `OnPrepareResponse` to `UseStaticFiles` that:
  - Sets `Cache-Control: no-cache, no-store, must-revalidate` (+ legacy `Pragma`, `Expires`) on `index.html`, `ngsw.json`, `ngsw-*` (the service-worker manifest must always be revalidated; otherwise PWA updates can stall).
  - Sets `Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable` on hashed `*.js / *.css / *.woff2 / *.woff` (Angular hashes filenames; safe to cache forever).
  - Sets no-cache on the StaticContent zone/page mount (it's mutable CMS content).
- **Why generic:** Real production gap. LightNap today serves all static files with default headers, so browsers either cache `index.html` (delaying PWA updates) or fail to long-cache hashed bundles. Generic best practice for any Angular-served-by-ASP.NET stack.
- **Files:** [src/LightNap.WebApi/Program.cs](src/LightNap.WebApi/Program.cs):120-140 area. Compare to Qwidgets's expanded version.
- **Classification:** Generic.
- **Recommendation:** Upstream.
- **Confidence:** High.
- **Risk & effort:** Low.
- **Depends on:** none.

**DECISION:** `[x] upstream`

---

## 4. Frontend

#### FE-01 ‚Äî **Bug fix:** version-check interval was `5 √ó 1000 √ó 1000 ms` (‚âà 83 min, not 5 min)

- **Area:** PWA service-worker update polling.
- **What changed:** LightNap [version-check.service.ts](src/lightnap-ng/src/app/core/services/version-check.service.ts):8 has `#updateCheckInterval = 5 * 1000 * 1000` with the comment `// 5 minutes`. That's actually 5 000 000 ms ‚âà 83 minutes. Qwidgets fixes it to `5 * 60 * 1000` (300 000 ms = real 5 minutes).
- **Why generic:** **Outright bug** that delays SW update detection by 16√ó.
- **Files:** [src/lightnap-ng/src/app/core/services/version-check.service.ts](src/lightnap-ng/src/app/core/services/version-check.service.ts):8.
- **Classification:** Generic. **Bug fix.**
- **Recommendation:** Upstream.
- **Confidence:** High.
- **Risk & effort:** Very low.
- **Depends on:** none.

**DECISION:** `[x] upstream`

#### FE-02 ‚Äî Version-check: silent activate-and-reload (vs. user-prompted drawer)

- **Area:** PWA update UX.
- **What changed:** Qwidgets removes the `versionUpdated$` subject and the "Application Update Available" drawer in `app.component`. Instead, on each SW `VERSION_READY` event it calls `swUpdate.activateUpdate().then(() => window.location.reload())` silently. Also: checks for updates **immediately on app startup** (LightNap waits for the first interval tick) and uses `registrationStrategy: "registerImmediately"` instead of `"registerWhenStable:30000"`.
- **Why generic:** Both behaviors are reasonable defaults ‚Äî opinion call. Pros of silent: simpler, no missed updates; cons: forced reload could interrupt user input. The "check on startup" change pairs with [FE-01](#fe-01--bug-fix-version-check-interval-was-5--1000--1000-ms--83-min-not-5-min) for total responsiveness.
- **Files:** [src/lightnap-ng/src/app/core/services/version-check.service.ts](src/lightnap-ng/src/app/core/services/version-check.service.ts), [src/lightnap-ng/src/app/app.component.ts](src/lightnap-ng/src/app/app.component.ts) (drawer removal), [src/lightnap-ng/src/app/app.component.html](src/lightnap-ng/src/app/app.component.html), [src/lightnap-ng/src/main.ts](src/lightnap-ng/src/main.ts):registrationStrategy.
- **Classification:** Generic.
- **Recommendation:** Needs maintainer judgment.
- **Confidence:** High.
- **Risk & effort:** Low.
- **Depends on:** FE-01.

**DECISION:** `[x] upstream`

#### FE-03 ‚Äî `ToastService.show(...)` gains optional `clickHandler`

- **Area:** Core toast service.
- **What changed:** Every overload (`show`, `success`, `info`, `error`, `warn`) now accepts an optional `clickHandler?: () => void`. The handler is stashed in `message.data.clickHandler` for the toast template.
- **Why generic:** "Click this toast to navigate / take action" is a common pattern.
- **Files:** [src/lightnap-ng/src/app/core/services/toast.service.ts](src/lightnap-ng/src/app/core/services/toast.service.ts).
- **Classification:** Generic.
- **Recommendation:** Upstream (paired with FE-04).
- **Confidence:** High.
- **Risk & effort:** Very low.
- **Depends on:** FE-04.

**DECISION:** `[x] upstream`

#### FE-04 ‚Äî Clickable toast template in `app.component.html`

- **Area:** App shell.
- **What changed:** `<p-toast>` now declares a custom template that branches on `message.data?.clickHandler` and adds `(click)="message.data.clickHandler()"` + `cursor-pointer` when present.
- **Why generic:** Renders the FE-03 capability.
- **Files:** [src/lightnap-ng/src/app/app.component.html](src/lightnap-ng/src/app/app.component.html):4-16.
- **Classification:** Generic.
- **Recommendation:** Upstream paired with FE-03.
- **Confidence:** High.
- **Risk & effort:** Very low.
- **Depends on:** FE-03.

**DECISION:** `[x] upstream`

#### FE-05 ‚Äî `<ln-api-response>` improvements (null-result fallback message + centered loading)

- **Area:** Core component.
- **What changed:** [api-response.component.html](src/lightnap-ng/src/app/core/components/api-response/api-response.component.html):4-7 originally only rendered `nullTemplateRef`; Qwidgets adds a `nullMessage()` fallback (`<p>{{ nullMessage() }}</p>`) when no template is provided. Also wraps the loading spinner+message in `<div class="w-full h-full flex flex-col items-center">` for proper centering.
- **Why generic:** Quality-of-life for a foundational UI primitive.
- **Files:** [src/lightnap-ng/src/app/core/components/api-response/api-response.component.html](src/lightnap-ng/src/app/core/components/api-response/api-response.component.html). The .ts also needs a `nullMessage = input<string|undefined>()` add ‚Äî confirm in [api-response.component.ts](src/lightnap-ng/src/app/core/components/api-response/api-response.component.ts).
- **Classification:** Generic.
- **Recommendation:** Upstream.
- **Confidence:** High.
- **Risk & effort:** Low.

#### FE-07 ‚Äî Breadcrumb component wrap + leading-icon fix

- **Area:** Layout.
- **What changed:** [breadcrumb.component.html](src/lightnap-ng/src/app/core/features/layout/components/controls/breadcrumb/breadcrumb.component.html) wraps each menu item in `<div class="text-nowrap">` to prevent ugly mid-label wraps, and now renders the optional `menuItem.icon` in *both* the router-link and the plain-span branches (LightNap only renders the icon in the plain-span case).
- **Why generic:** Bug + polish for layout component.
- **Files:** [src/lightnap-ng/src/app/core/features/layout/components/controls/breadcrumb/breadcrumb.component.html](src/lightnap-ng/src/app/core/features/layout/components/controls/breadcrumb/breadcrumb.component.html).

#### FE-08 ‚Äî `app-layout` page-actions slot (sticky floating actions on md+, collapsible on sm)

- **Area:** App layout.
- **What changed:** Qwidgets adds a `pageActionsTemplate` signal to `LayoutService` (a `TemplateRef`). When a page sets it, the layout renders the template (a) inline under the breadcrumb on small screens with a chevron toggle, (b) in a fixed floating panel top-right on md+ screens. The companion `PageActionsDirective` lets a child set the template just by declaring `<ng-template appPageActions>...</ng-template>`.
- **Why generic:** Page-actions slots are a generic UX pattern ‚Äî currently the layout offers no mechanism for a child route to surface actions outside the page body.
- **Files:** [app-layout.component.html](src/lightnap-ng/src/app/core/features/layout/components/layouts/app-layout/app-layout.component.html), [app-layout.component.ts](src/lightnap-ng/src/app/core/features/layout/components/layouts/app-layout/app-layout.component.ts). The directive itself is new in Qwidgets under `core/features/layout/directives/page-actions.directive.ts` (verify before upstreaming).
- **Classification:** Generic.
- **Recommendation:** Needs maintainer judgment ‚Äî useful primitive but the LayoutService surface grows.
- **Confidence:** High.
- **Risk & effort:** Medium.
- **Depends on:** FE-09.

**DECISION:** `[x] upstream`

#### FE-09 ‚Äî Modernize `AppLayoutComponent` DI (use `inject()` + `viewChild.required()`)

- **Area:** App layout.
- **What changed:** Constructor injection (`constructor(public layoutService, public renderer, public router)`) replaced with field-level `readonly layoutService = inject(LayoutService); readonly #renderer = inject(Renderer2); readonly #router = inject(Router);`. `@ViewChild(AppSidebarComponent) appSidebar!` replaced with `readonly appSidebar = viewChild.required(AppSidebarComponent);` (Angular signals-based query, type-safe required-by-default).
- **Why generic:** Standard modern-Angular refactor; aligns with the rest of LightNap which already mostly uses `inject()`.
- **Files:** [src/lightnap-ng/src/app/core/features/layout/components/layouts/app-layout/app-layout.component.ts](src/lightnap-ng/src/app/core/features/layout/components/layouts/app-layout/app-layout.component.ts).
- **Classification:** Generic.
- **Recommendation:** Upstream.
- **Confidence:** High.
- **Risk & effort:** Low.
- **Depends on:** none.

**DECISION:** `[x] upstream`

#### FE-10 ‚Äî `TypeHelpers` gains `objectsEqual`, `stripUndefined`, `rowTrackBy`

- **Area:** Core helpers.
- **What changed:** [type-helpers.ts](src/lightnap-ng/src/app/core/helpers/type-helpers.ts) gains three static methods:
  ```ts
  static objectsEqual(obj1: any, obj2: any): boolean    // JSON.stringify compare
  static stripUndefined<T>(obj: T): T                    // JSON round-trip
  static rowTrackBy<T>(selector: (item: T) => string)    // trackBy factory
  ```
- **Why generic:** All three are tiny, useful, framework-agnostic helpers.
- **Files:** [src/lightnap-ng/src/app/core/helpers/type-helpers.ts](src/lightnap-ng/src/app/core/helpers/type-helpers.ts).
- **Classification:** Generic.
- **Recommendation:** Upstream.

- **Risk & effort:** Very low.
- **Depends on:** none.

**DECISION:** `[x] upstream`

#### FE-11 ‚Äî `PrependNameTitleStrategy` skips prepend if page title already contains `|`

- **Area:** Router title strategy.
- **What changed:** [prepend-name-title.strategy.ts](src/lightnap-ng/src/app/core/strategies/prepend-name-title.strategy.ts):11 ‚Äî `if (this.#appName?.length)` becomes `if (this.#appName?.length && !title.includes("|"))`. Lets a page that wants a custom title (e.g. `"My Order #123 | MyApp"`) override the default `"MyApp | My Order #123"` prefix.
- **Why generic:** Useful escape hatch.
- **Files:** [src/lightnap-ng/src/app/core/strategies/prepend-name-title.strategy.ts](src/lightnap-ng/src/app/core/strategies/prepend-name-title.strategy.ts).

- **Recommendation:** Upstream.

- **Risk & effort:** Very low.
- **Depends on:** none.

**DECISION:** `[x] upstream`

#### FE-12 ‚Äî Add `@features` / `@integrations` path aliases in `tsconfig.json`

- **Area:** TypeScript path mapping.
- **What changed:** [tsconfig.json](src/lightnap-ng/tsconfig.json):20-24 only had `@core` / `@testing` aliases. Qwidgets adds `@features` / `@features/*` (for `src/app/features`) and `@integrations` / `@integrations/*` (for `src/integrations`).
- **Why generic:** Path aliases are a generic project-structure convenience; adding `@features` matches the existing `@core` pattern. (LightNap doesn't currently have a `features/` folder at the app level ‚Äî adding the alias is harmless but unused until something lives there.)
- **Files:** [src/lightnap-ng/tsconfig.json](src/lightnap-ng/tsconfig.json).
- **Classification:** Generic (alias) / Gray-area (the alias is dead code if no `features/` folder exists).
- **Recommendation:** Needs maintainer judgment ‚Äî adopt as soon as you create a `features/` folder.
- **Confidence:** High.
- **Risk & effort:** Very low.
- **Depends on:** none.

**DECISION:** `[x] upstream`

#### FE-13 ‚Äî Exclude `/sitemap*`, `/sitemaps/**`, `/robots.txt` from PWA navigation routes

- **Area:** Service worker.
- **What changed:** [ngsw-config.json](src/lightnap-ng/ngsw-config.json):29 `navigationUrls` was `["/**", "!/api/**", "!/swagger/**", "!/signin-*"]`. Qwidgets adds `"!/sitemap*", "!/sitemaps/**", "!/robots.txt"`. Without this, the SW intercepts `/robots.txt` and `/sitemap.xml` and serves `index.html` instead ‚Äî silently breaking SEO crawling.
- **Why generic:** Real bug for any LightNap consumer that ever sets up SEO.
- **Files:** [src/lightnap-ng/ngsw-config.json](src/lightnap-ng/ngsw-config.json):29.
- **Classification:** Generic. **Bug fix.**
- **Recommendation:** Upstream.
- **Confidence:** High.
- **Risk & effort:** Very low.
- **Depends on:** none.

**DECISION:** `[x] upstream`

#### FE-14 ‚Äî Barrel-import migration (`@core` ‚Üí specific paths)

- **Area:** TypeScript imports across `src/app/`.
- **What changed:** Many files migrated from `import { RoutePipe, AppRoute, RoleNames, ApiResponseDto, NotificationDto, ... } from "@core"` (which re-exports everything) to specific path imports like `import { AppRoute } from "@core/features/routing/models/app-route"`, `import { RoutePipe } from "@core/features/routing/pipes/route.pipe"`, `import { NotificationDto } from "@core/backend-api"`, `import { setApiErrors } from "@core/helpers"`. Confirmed across `pages/identity/*/*.ts`, `pages/profile/*/*.ts`, `pages/admin/roles/*.ts`, `pages/home/routes.ts`, `pages/public/routes.ts`, `app/pages/routes.ts`, `core/components/error-list/error-list.component.ts`, `core/strategies/prepend-name-title.strategy.ts`, `core/services/public.service.ts`, `core/features/layout/services/menu.service.ts`, etc.
- **Why generic:** Better tree-shaking, faster builds, and clearer refactor signal. Modern Angular guidance favors specific imports over barrel-style.
- **Files:** ~30 `.ts` files. Comprehensive find-and-replace candidate.
- **Classification:** Generic.
- **Recommendation:** Needs maintainer judgment ‚Äî the change is mechanical but spans many files. Could be done incrementally.
- **Confidence:** High.
- **Risk & effort:** Medium effort (many files); low risk (mechanical).
- **Depends on:** none.

**DECISION:** `[x] defer`

  .p-breadcrumb-list { flex-wrap: wrap !important; display: flex !important; }

- **Area:** Markdown rendering.

The extension itself rewrites anchor tags for non-same-origin URLs to include `target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"`.

## 5. Roll-up: suggested upstreaming order

Sequenced so each step is small, testable, and unblocks the next. (Not a commitment ‚Äî just a suggested order.)

1. **Bug fixes** (no surface change, smallest review): BE-06 (email IsBodyHtml), BE-23 (Dockerfile .NET 10), FE-01 (version-check interval), FE-13 (ngsw sitemap exclusions).
2. **Patch-level dep bumps**: DEP-01, DEP-02, DEP-04, DEP-09, DEP-10, DEP-12.
3. **Generic refactors with no surface change**: BE-07, BE-08, BE-09, BE-10, BE-11, BE-13, BE-24, FE-05, FE-06, FE-07, FE-10, FE-11, FE-15, FE-16.
4. **DI / bootstrap improvements**: BE-02, BE-04, BE-16, BE-17, BE-20, BE-21, BE-22.
5. **PWA + static-file caching**: BE-14, BE-15, FE-02 (paired with FE-01), FE-03+FE-04.
6. **DataProtection** (new subsystem): BE-03 + DEP-08 + appsettings changes + BE-26 (template).
7. **Larger refactors**: FE-09, FE-08, BE-05 (hub rename ‚Äî coordinate with frontend hub URL), DEP-11 (orval major + regen).
8. **Optional / debatable**: BE-01 (Qwidgets.Configuration extraction), BE-12 (OptionDto breaking rename), BE-18 (MarketingOptIn entity fields), BE-19 (extensible OAuth), BE-25 (file-scoped namespace cleanup), FE-12 (path aliases), FE-14 (barrel-import migration), FE-17 (main.css layout strategy), FE-18+DEP-14 (markdown external links).
9. **Skip in this pass**: DEP-05/06/07 (sanitization / Markdig / AppInsights ‚Äî adopt later if you adopt the consumer features).

## 6. Excluded ‚Äî domain-specific Qwidgets changes (not candidates)

Counts only, per scope, so coverage is visible without itemizing prediction-market features.

| Domain area | Excluded items (counts) | Brief note |
|---|---:|---|
| **Prediction-market exchanges** | 4 integration libs + 4 WebJobs + ~120 files | `src/Integrations/{Kalshi,Polymarket,PredictIt,Manifold}/Qwidgets.Integrations.<Provider>(.<Provider>WebJob)/` ‚Äî full client/streaming/cache/account/data integrations for each venue. |
| **AI integrations** | ~10 files | `src/Integrations/Anthropic/Qwidgets.Integrations.Anthropic/Claude*` + `src/Integrations/Google/Qwidgets.Integrations.Google/Gemini*` (LLM clients). |
| **Predictions subsystem** | ~25 files | `src/Qwidgets.Core/Predictions/*`, `src/Qwidgets.Configuration/Models/PredictionSearch*Settings.cs` + matching `Qwidgets.Configuration/Services/*PredictionSearchSnapshot*` (Lucene-backed search index, Azure-blob snapshot reader/writer). |
| **Workspaces / SharedWorkspaces** | ~30 files | `src/Qwidgets.Core/Workspaces/`, `src/Qwidgets.Core/SharedWorkspaces/`, the `Workspace`/`Widget` EF entities, related controllers/services, the workspace-sharing migrations (`20260326205012_AddWorkspaceSharingFields`, `20260331221942_AddShareImageUrl`). |
| **Widgets** | ~5 files | Widget EF entity + SEO providers. |
| **Telemetry deep wiring** | ~10 files | `src/Qwidgets.Core/Telemetry/*`, `src/Qwidgets.WebApi/Middleware/TelemetryMiddleware.cs`, `src/Qwidgets.WebApi/Services/TelemetryContextService.cs`, `MaintenanceTaskTelemetryWrapper`. **Gray-area** at the boundary ‚Äî concept is generic, Qwidgets's `ITelemetryService` is concrete-coupled to ApplicationInsights and to domain events. |
| **SEO middleware + sitemap** | ~10 files | `src/Qwidgets.WebApi/Middleware/OpenGraphMiddleware.cs`, `src/Qwidgets.WebApi/Middleware/Seo/*` (5 widget SEO providers + resolver), `src/Qwidgets.WebApi/Services/SitemapService.cs`, `src/Qwidgets.WebApi/Controllers/SitemapController.cs`. **Gray-area** ‚Äî the OpenGraph render-on-server middleware is conceptually generic; the SEO providers are domain. |
| **Domain controllers in WebApi** | 9 controllers | `AdminWorkspacesController`, `AiController`, `FeedController`, `IntegrationsController`, `PredictionsAccountController`, `PredictionsDataController`, `PredictionsSearchController`, `SharedController`, `SitemapController`, `WorkspacesController`. |
| **Frontend domain pages/components/services** | ~150 files | `qwidgets-ng/src/app/pages/{predictions,predictions-account,workspaces,integrations,shared,...}/`, `core/features/{workspaces,integrations,predictions,...}/`, `core/components/{chart,query,radio-option,toggle-option,top-action-bar,...}` (some are gray-area generic ‚Äî see ‚öÝ below), Highcharts wiring, Gridster wiring, Quill, etc. |
| **Frontend domain deps** | 7 packages | `highcharts`, `highcharts-angular`, `angular-gridster2`, `quill`, `@tanstack/angular-query-experimental`, `@microsoft/applicationinsights-web`, `@microsoft/applicationinsights-angularplugin-js`. |
| **Brand/copy** | many | LightNap ‚Üí Qwidgets logos/copyright/title/manifest/atom-feed/`href`-to-quantcha.com. Not candidates. |

‚öÝ **Gray-area items observed but not itemized as candidates** (the maintainer may want to look at them individually later):

- `qwidgets-ng/src/app/core/components/{chart,query,radio-option,toggle-option,top-action-bar}/` ‚Äî looks generic by name but **inspection of file contents was outside the diff window**; some are likely domain (chart is Highcharts-coupled), some may be reusable (radio-option, toggle-option, top-action-bar).
- `qwidgets-ng/src/app/core/directives/{cta,fallback-image}.directive.ts` ‚Äî `fallback-image` (swap broken image for placeholder) is *probably* generic; `cta` (call-to-action analytics) is *probably* domain.
- `qwidgets-ng/src/app/core/helpers/{color,math,query-client}-helpers.ts` ‚Äî `math-helpers` likely generic; `color-helpers` and `query-client-helpers` look generic.
- `qwidgets-ng/src/app/core/pipes/{change-percent,change,short-number}.pipe.ts` ‚Äî pipes are framework-agnostic; could be candidates.
- `qwidgets-ng/src/app/core/services/{session-state,ux}.service.ts` ‚Äî names suggest generic; not inspected in this pass.
- `qwidgets-ng/src/app/core/guards/auth-resolved.guard.ts` ‚Äî name suggests generic guard pattern (wait for auth-init before activating); not inspected.

> **OQ-2.** Should a follow-up pass inspect the gray-area items above and split each into "generic core helper" vs "domain"? They look promising at a glance but the auto-script's per-file inspection didn't reach them.

## 7. Open questions

1. **OQ-1** (above): Does the `Scaffolding/` subsystem removal in Qwidgets indicate a future deprecation, or is it Qwidgets-specific? Action: none for this report.
2. **OQ-2** (above): Should the gray-area frontend core helpers be inspected file-by-file in a follow-up pass?
3. **OQ-3:** Qwidgets removed `WindowsAuthSettings` and the underlying Windows authentication setup. Generic-or-domain? ‚Äî Could be either: a starter kit *might* want to offer the option, but Qwidgets's decision suggests it's dead weight in practice. Not flagged as a candidate (it would be a *removal* upstream, and only if you're sure no one uses it).
4. **OQ-4:** Several **gray-area** items (BE-01, BE-07/DEP-03, FE-08, etc.) depend on each other. The maintainer might want to upstream the foundational pieces (BE-02, BE-04, BE-09, BE-13, etc.) first, then re-evaluate the bigger refactors with fresh eyes.
